place: Nathon, Ko Samui District, Surat Thani, Thailand
title: Cliff-Hanging Infinity-Pool Sports Villa, Nathon
blurb: A sport-and-leisure retreat built into the Nathon hillside, where a 14-metre infinity pool hangs over the cliff edge above the Gulf — every room framing the sea, an indoor gym, putting green and a full games lawn waiting just beyond the deck.
detail:
Built onto the Nathon hillside with a breathtaking panorama of the surrounding vistas and sea, this is an exclusive hideaway conceived for large groups who want for nothing. An opulent entrance leads past a pond and tropical garden, through an ornate hand-carved wooden door from Chiang Mai, and opens to an indoor living room with a pool table and a dining space for ten — which in turn spills out to the deck, the pool and the spectacular surroundings beyond.

The headline is a 14-metre infinity pool that appears to spill straight off the cliff toward the horizon, with sun beds lined along its edge and a master-suite jacuzzi above. While the view from every room is one of nature's finest works, the villa's amenities compete for attention: an indoor gym, a putting green, a lawn marked out for badminton, volleyball or football, and even a petanque boulodrome for target bowling.

Arranged over two floors with three to six-plus mezzanine bedrooms, the home suits multi-generational gatherings, reunions and intimate weddings of up to twenty. A villa manager, private chef and housekeeping team look after the stay, with a welcome drink, daily breakfast and high-speed WiFi throughout — and Bang Por's quiet beaches and the north-shore restaurants only a short drive away.
